long_term_goal: In 3 months, the Alexa Expression System is a fully realised, generative-ready design foundation that DxD teams can scale independently.
sprint_questions: Can we define what 'evolving the seeds' means in practice without access to the seeds themselves?
Will the RFP scope constraints satisfy DxD's actual needs?
Can we identify the critical gaps that must be filled before design work begins?
key_question_answered: Critical gaps identified: seed review, engineer-consumable format, adoption testing. Sprint 001 is blocked until seeds are provided.
key_decision: Established three non-negotiable requirements: seed review gates Sprint 001, deliverables must be engineer-consumable, adoption testing is the success metric.
elias_dissent: System-level definition may prioritise partner constraints over user needs. DxD needs usable components, not abstract definitions. Review trigger: prototype user testing shows adoption struggles.
next_action: DxD Team to provide internal seeds (shape morphology, motion, generative behaviour concepts) for squad review.
open_questions: What is the maturity level of the internal seeds?
What are the actual rendering budgets for Echo Spot and Frames?
What prototyping tools will we use?
How do we test adoption during the engagement?
